rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MATSUYE MIYAMOTO KAWASE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Order To Appear For Hearing On Whether To Remove Administrator
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. This matter will be heard with PES-14-298170. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 7th Amended General Order which took effect on 10/30/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KWAN SOOK LAU
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/15/20. Petitioner requests approval of accounting, but waivers of accounting have been filed. Prior to the review of the petition, Peitioner will need to either file an amendment to the petition requesting waiver of accounting or provide bank statements to verify cash on hand pursuant to Local Rule 14.62. Filed stamped copy of any pleading filed and a proposed order must be delivered to Room 202 at least 2 weeks prior.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES PINGFOON LAU
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/22/2020. The following issues must be addressed: 1) No proposed orders have been received. Please mail paper copies to Room 202. 2) No courtesy copy of the petition has been received. Please mail it to Room 202. 3) The petition must be verified by the petitioner personally. Probate Code section 1023 & CRC 7.103(c). See also LRSF 14.18B re omitted verification. 4) Has the petitioner taken any IAEA actions for which notice of proposed action was required? If so, please list and describe all such actions. CRC 7.250(a). 5) Has the petitioner given notice to any public entity listed in Probate Code sections 9201 & 9202? 6) Is the real property listed on the inventory and appraisal the decedent's one-half share of community property or his separate property? Probate Code section 8850(c). 7) Please describe the steps mentioned in paragraph 5 of the petition and required to petition for final distribution. Also, why does the petitioner need one year to close this estate? 8) What are the names and addresses of the decedent's parents? Notice of this petition must be given to them. If both parents are deceased, please state their date of death. 9) If the decedent's parents are both deceased, what are the names and addresses of issue of the parents within the meaning of Probate Code section 6402(c)? Notice of this petition must be given to them.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALICIA M. HOPKINSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required by both attorney and personal representative. Issues to include: 1) Petitioner states that "under her full IAEA authority, the executor listed the property for sale and directed that any sales proceeds be delivered to the Hopkinson 2009 Trust" and "In accordance with the instructions of the Executor, Edwin Dyer, the trustee of the Keith Hopkinson 2009 Trust, distributed the proportionate share of the proceeds of one-half of the proceeds, minus expenses of sale, to each of the beneficiaries named in the Trust." Thus, it appears that not only were estate assets distributed out of this estate without an order, it was also not distributed to the beneficiary of this estate (i.e. the personal representative of Keith Hopkinson's estate). Consequently, there are no assets to distribute. 2) Petitioner alleges that $12,500 was paid to attorney out of escrow at the time of completion of sale, which has been placed in the attorney's Client Trust Account, and the additional statutory compensation due of $3,000 has been provided for. Remote appearances are highly encouraged. Please review the 7th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 7th amended general order. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FIRST AND LAST NAME.
